summary: All 10 review-request tests PASS. Design-token migration from terracotta to muted olive (#3F5F4A / rgb(63,95,74)) verified across all pages. Body bg is rgb(247,244,239) (#F7F4EF warm off-white) site-wide. Button radius=14px, card radius=20px both confirmed. Manrope replaces JetBrains Mono via .font-mono-tight class. Backend pytest (6 tests) passes in 9.6s. The two visual regressions flagged in iteration_52 (dark CTA band on homepage + dark UnreadableListing container) are BOTH FIXED — homepage scan returns zero dark sections; unreadable-screen bg is now rgb(249,248,246).

issues: The 'Below Market' price-position label and the 'VERIFIED' status badges render in rgb(125,191,143) — a brighter sage/mint green, not the new primary muted-olive token rgb(63,95,74). This is in the green family (olive-adjacent) and likely intentional as a separate 'positive status' color, so all tests pass under the 'olive-family color' criterion in TEST 4, but it is worth confirming whether status-positive should align more tightly with the new primary accent for full visual cohesion.

action_items: Optional cohesion polish: consider tightening the 'positive status' color (currently rgb(125,191,143)) to a deeper olive that ladders into the new primary accent rgb(63,95,74), so VERIFIED badges and 'Below Market' label share the visual family of the CTA buttons. Pure design polish, not a functional issue.
critical_code_review_comments: Design tokens are now consumed cleanly — no more literal espresso hex leftover on UnreadableListing or homepage CTA band (both fixed since iter52). Token-driven theming is working as intended.
Backend server.py still ~4300 lines — the invest parser block could be split into a dedicated module; carried over from iter51/iter52 (not in scope of this iteration).
Console still shows recurring 401s on every page load from a third-party resource — unchanged from iter52, cosmetic only.

success_rate.frontend: 100% functional + 100% design intent (1 optional polish note on status-positive shade)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter53 design-token migration (terracotta -> muted olive #3F5F4A, JetBrains Mono -> Manrope via .font-mono-tight remap, body bg #F9F8F6 -> #F7F4EF, card radius 16->20px, button radius pill->14px) is fully verified. All 10 review tests pass: homepage body bg rgb(247,244,239), CTA bg rgb(63,95,74) radius 14px, 3 proof cards white bg radius 20px, accent span 'for Hospitality Assets' color rgb(63,95,74), nav brand dot olive, invest dashboard fully light + decision strip populated, operate hero + tiles visible, dashboard 5 KPIs + watchlist + pulse, portfolio + changes, unreadable screen bg rgb(249,248,246) + 4 paths, backend parse-text returns Athens/Koukaki/280000/65/2 correctly, document.title contains 'PropOS', no VELA/STAYOS strings, propos-nav/propos-footer present. The two iter52 dark-surface regressions are CONFIRMED FIXED. Only minor optional polish: VERIFIED badges + 'Below Market' use sage-green rgb(125,191,143) which is olive-adjacent but lighter than the new primary accent.
rca of the issue: No bugs found this iteration. The design token migration is clean — variable-only theming worked across all components including the two surfaces that hard-coded espresso hexes in iter52.
